# Justin Notarfrancesco

> Justin Notarfrancesco is a senior frontend engineer in New York City. He has
> spent five years building financial interfaces at JPMorgan Chase and
> specializes in fintech-grade frontend engineering: design systems, WCAG
> accessibility, data visualization, and UIs where money math must reconcile
> to the cent.

Site: https://notar.nyc — a portfolio styled after the 1970 NYC Transit
Authority Standards Manual, built with Astro as static HTML.

## Pages

- [About](https://notar.nyc/about): background, skills, and tech stack
- [Work](https://notar.nyc/projects): selected projects with full write-ups
- [Contact](https://notar.nyc/contact): email, GitHub, and LinkedIn

## Selected projects

- [Idle](https://notar.nyc/projects/idle): treasury automation for startups —
  T-bill ladders, maker-checker transfer controls, and a to-the-cent ledger
  priced from live U.S. Treasury data (Next.js, TypeScript, property-based tests)
- [Last Stop](https://notar.nyc/projects/last-stop): a daily NYC-subway arcade
  game in vanilla TypeScript and canvas
- [Can My Cat Eat XYZ](https://notar.nyc/projects/can-my-cat-eat-xyz): a
  single-purpose cat-nutrition answer engine on the OpenAI API
- [notar.nyc](https://notar.nyc/projects/this-site): this site

## Elsewhere

- GitHub: https://github.com/justin-notarfrancesco
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/justin-notarfrancesco/
